---
title: "Topic Analysis"
author: "John Orroth"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: pdf_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

```{r}
# install.packages(c("tidyverse", "tidytext", "lubridate", "scales",
#                    "topicmodels", "tm", "stringr", "forcats", "ggridges"))

#install.packages("ggridges")
library(tidyverse)
library(tidytext)
library(lubridate)
library(scales)
library(topicmodels)
library(tm)
library(stringr)
library(forcats)
library(ggridges)

trump_posts <- read.csv("filtered_trump_dat.csv")
```

```{r}
# Replace this with your actual file read if needed
# trump_posts <- read_csv("trump_posts.csv")

trump_posts <- trump_posts %>%
  mutate(
    date = ymd_hms(date),
    year = year(date),
    month = floor_date(date, unit = "month"),
    platform = as.factor(platform),
    deleted_flag = as.logical(deleted_flag)
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(text), text != "")

# Basic text cleaning before tokenization
trump_clean <- trump_posts %>%
  mutate(
    text_clean = text %>%
      str_to_lower() %>%
      str_replace_all("http[s]?://\\S+", " ") %>%     # remove urls
      str_replace_all("www\\.\\S+", " ") %>%
      str_replace_all("&amp;", " ") %>%
      str_replace_all("[^a-z\\s']", " ") %>%          # keep letters/apostrophes
      str_replace_all("\\s+", " ") %>%
      str_trim()
  )

# Custom words you may want to remove because they dominate but add little meaning
custom_stop <- tibble(
  word = c("trump", "donald", "realdonaldtrump", "amp", "rt", "will", "just",
           "today", "tonight", "tomorrow", "one", "two", "new", "great")
)

tokens <- trump_clean %>%
  select(X, date, year, month, platform, text_clean) %>%
  unnest_tokens(word, text_clean) %>%
  filter(str_detect(word, "[a-z]")) %>%
  anti_join(stop_words, by = "word") %>%
  anti_join(custom_stop, by = "word") %>%
  filter(nchar(word) >= 3)

head(tokens)
```

```{r}
yearly_word_counts <- tokens %>%
  count(year, word, sort = TRUE)

head(yearly_word_counts)

yearly_word_props <- yearly_word_counts %>%
  group_by(year) %>%
  mutate(
    total_words_year = sum(n),
    prop = n / total_words_year,
    per_10k = prop * 10000
  ) %>%
  ungroup()

head(yearly_word_props)

top_words_overall <- tokens %>%
  count(word, sort = TRUE) %>%
  slice_max(n, n = 20)

ggplot(top_words_overall, aes(x = reorder(word, n), y = n)) +
  geom_col(fill = "steelblue", width = 0.8) +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(
    title = "Most Frequent Words Across All Trump Posts",
    x = NULL,
    y = "Word Count"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14)
```


```{r}
top_20_words <- tokens %>%
  count(word, sort = TRUE) %>%
  slice_max(n, n = 20) %>%
  pull(word)

heatmap_data <- yearly_word_props %>%
  filter(word %in% top_20_words)

ggplot(heatmap_data, aes(x = year, y = fct_reorder(word, per_10k), fill = per_10k)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white") +
  scale_fill_gradient(low = "#dbe9f6", high = "#08306b", labels = comma) +
  labs(
    title = "Top Word Frequency Over Time",
    subtitle = "Frequency shown as occurrences per 10,000 words each year",
    x = "Year",
    y = NULL,
    fill = "Per 10k words"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13)
```
```{r}
selected_words <- c("america", "country", "people", "democrats", "republicans",
                    "border", "china", "jobs", "fake", "election")

trend_data <- yearly_word_props %>%
  filter(word %in% selected_words)

ggplot(trend_data, aes(x = year, y = per_10k, color = word)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1.1) +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(min(trend_data$year), max(trend_data$year), by = 1)) +
  labs(
    title = "Selected Word Trends Over Time",
    subtitle = "Occurrences per 10,000 words",
    x = "Year",
    y = "Per 10k words",
    color = "Word"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))
```


```{r}
platform_word_props <- tokens %>%
  count(year, platform, word) %>%
  group_by(year, platform) %>%
  mutate(
    total_words = sum(n),
    prop = n / total_words,
    per_10k = prop * 10000
  ) %>%
  ungroup()

platform_trend <- platform_word_props %>%
  filter(word %in% c("america", "china", "border", "election", "media"))

ggplot(platform_trend, aes(x = year, y = per_10k, color = platform)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  facet_wrap(~word, scales = "free_y") +
  labs(
    title = "Word Usage Over Time by Platform",
    subtitle = "Comparing Twitter and Truth Social",
    x = "Year",
    y = "Per 10k words",
    color = "Platform"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13)
```


```{r}
monthly_word_props <- tokens %>%
  count(month, word) %>%
  group_by(month) %>%
  mutate(
    total_words = sum(n),
    prop = n / total_words,
    per_10k = prop * 10000
  ) %>%
  ungroup()

monthly_election <- monthly_word_props %>%
  filter(word %in% c("election", "china", "border", "media"))

ggplot(monthly_election, aes(x = month, y = per_10k, color = word)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
  labs(
    title = "Monthly Word Frequency Over Time",
    subtitle = "Occurrences per 10,000 words",
    x = "Month",
    y = "Per 10k words",
    color = "Word"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13)
```

tfidf

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(tidytext)

year_word_tfidf <- tokens %>%
  count(year, word, sort = TRUE) %>%
  bind_tf_idf(word, year, n) %>%
  arrange(desc(tf_idf))

top_tfidf_by_year <- year_word_tfidf %>%
  group_by(year) %>%
  slice_max(tf_idf, n = 8, with_ties = FALSE) %>%
  ungroup()

# Create 4-year groups
year_groups <- tibble(
  year = sort(unique(top_tfidf_by_year$year))
) %>%
  mutate(group = ceiling(row_number() / 4))

# Join groups back to the tf-idf data
top_tfidf_by_year_grouped <- top_tfidf_by_year %>%
  left_join(year_groups, by = "year")

# Split into a list of data frames, one per 4-year chunk
plot_list <- top_tfidf_by_year_grouped %>%
  group_split(group)

# Make one plot for each chunk
for (i in seq_along(plot_list)) {
  
  plot_data <- plot_list[[i]] %>%
    mutate(word = reorder_within(word, tf_idf, year))
  
  p <- ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = tf_idf, y = word)) +
    geom_col(fill = "steelblue") +
    facet_wrap(~year, scales = "free_y", ncol = 2) +
    scale_y_reordered() +
    labs(
      title = paste("Top TF-IDF Words by Year: Group", i),
      x = "TF-IDF",
      y = NULL
    ) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 13)
  
  print(p)
}
```
